name: python3-fuzzywuzzy
title: python3-fuzzywuzzy
summary: Python 3 module for fuzzy string matching
description:
Python 3 module for fuzzy string matching
Various methods for fuzzy matching of strings in Python, including:
.
- String similarity: Gives a measure of string similarity between 0 and 100.
- Partial string similarity: Inconsistent substrings are a common problem
when string matching. To get around it, use a "best partial" heuristic
when two strings are of noticeably different lengths.
- Token sort: This approach involves tokenizing the string in question,
sorting the tokens alphabetically, and then joining them back into a
string.
- Token set: A slightly more flexible approach. Tokenize both strings, but
instead of immediately sorting and comparing, split the tokens into two
groups: intersection and remainder.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-fuzzywuzzy because Python 3 module for fuzzy string matching.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-fuzzywuzzy as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-fuzzywuzzy with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-fuzzywuzzy --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
